label: Four of five analogies collapse to weighted selection or bundleRules; only load-balancer health-check and DNS TTL add a new primitive
Each mode has a runtime health score; routing weight = staticWeight × healthMultiplier
Four structural costs health-check hides: prompt-dependent state, grader cost, cold-start, Goodhart
The genuine transfer is the feedback channel (routeId + re-prompt signal), smaller than the health-check analogy
Closed-loop routing supplies the measurement infrastructure run-2 said was missing

direction: OS scheduler priority/preemption as a routing primitive
reason: Priority=today's weight; preemption incoherent for one-shot routing; aging collapses to learned weights

direction: IP longest-prefix match as a routing primitive
reason: vocabularyClasses already is longest-prefix match by another name

direction: Receptionist ask-don't-guess as a routing primitive
reason: Already shipped as defer; triage protocol is bundleRules

direction: Full health-score routing as immediate destination
reason: F3 four costs make it multi-release, not refactor

note: Handoff is a typed closed-loop signal; strictly more informative than re-prompt. Iteration 3 should stress handoff as the primitive, not just as a way to dissolve keep-vs-null.

idea: Closed-loop routing: emit routeId, record whether the next operator turn re-enters the router; this is the minimal feedback channel
